info.title: avrolution
info.description: Schema validation, compatibility, impact analysis, and code generation for Avro workflows.

x-inspectra.cliParsedDescription:
Avrolution CLI

Examples:
    avrolution validate schema.avsc
    avrolution validate --profile strict < schema.avsc
    avrolution compare old.avsc new.avsc --mode full
    avrolution impact old.avsc new.avsc
    avrolution infer sample.json
    avrolution codegen schema.avsc --out generated\

commands.name: validate
commands.description:
Validate an AVSC file.

Examples:
    avrolution validate user.avsc
    avrolution validate --profile strict user.avsc
    Get-Content user.avsc | avrolution validate --inline-only

Exit codes:
    0 = schema is valid (no Error diagnostics)
    1 = validation errors or invalid arguments

commands.name: compare
commands.description:
Compare two AVSC files.

Examples:
    avrolution compare v1.avsc v2.avsc
    avrolution compare v1.avsc v2.avsc --mode backward
    avrolution compare v1.avsc v2.avsc --mode full --profile strict --inline-only
    avrolution compare --old-text "{"type":"record",...}" --new-text "{"type":"record",...}"

Exit codes:
    0 = selected compatibility mode passes and no medium/high risk changes
    1 = selected compatibility mode fails (or overall NONE/ERROR in overall mode)
    2 = compatibility passes but medium/high risk changes exist

commands.name: impact
commands.description:
Analyze impact between two AVSC files.

Examples:
    avrolution impact v1.avsc v2.avsc
    avrolution impact v1.avsc v2.avsc --profile strict --inline-only
    avrolution impact --old-text "{"type":"record",...}" --new-text "{"type":"record",...}"

Exit codes:
    0 = no medium/high/breaking impacts
    1 = at least one breaking impact
    2 = no breaking impacts, but medium/high impacts exist

commands.name: infer
commands.description:
Infer AVSC schema from JSON payload.

Examples:
    avrolution infer sample.json
    Get-Content sample.json | avrolution infer --stdin

Exit codes:
    0 = infer succeeded
    1 = invalid arguments or infer failure

commands.name: codegen
commands.description:
Generate code from AVSC.

Examples:
    avrolution codegen user.avsc
    avrolution codegen user.avsc --out User.g.cs
    avrolution codegen user.avsc --out generated\
    avrolution codegen user.avsc --lang java --out User.java
    avrolution codegen user.avsc --lang python --out user.py
    avrolution codegen user.avsc --lang rust --out user.rs
    avrolution codegen user.avsc --lang typescript --out user.ts
    avrolution codegen user.avsc --lang go --out user.go
    avrolution codegen user.avsc --lang kotlin --out User.kt
    Get-Content user.avsc | avrolution codegen --stdin --lang csharp

Namespace behavior:
    Uses namespace from AVSC when present.
    Uses fallback namespace when AVSC has no namespace.

commands.name: apply
commands.description:
Apply suggestions from a plan to an AVSC schema.

Examples:
    avrolution apply schema.avsc --plan plan.json
    avrolution apply schema.avsc --plan plan.json --ids SUG-001,SUG-002
    avrolution apply schema.avsc --plan plan.json --kinds AddDefaultNull
    avrolution apply schema.avsc --plan plan.json --only nonbreaking --dry-run

commands.name: guard
commands.description:
Evaluate schema evolution policy guardrails.

Examples:
    avrolution guard new.avsc --against old.avsc
    avrolution guard new.avsc --against old.avsc --profile strict --inline-only --json

Exit codes:
    0 = pass
    1 = policy violation
    2 = schema invalid
    3 = guard execution error
